title: Automations management / sort / print list?

posts.author: EBChartrand (Eric B Chartrand)
posts.cookedHtml:
Asked support for this, and was told that it’s not possible at the moment.
Wish there was a way to manage / sort automation rules
Where we could group by category or at least slide up or down within the list to either set priority or group by same type of action or whatever. a way to copy-paste rules between boards
And once at it, get a printout of the automations of a board

Trying to troubleshoot automations on a board started by a co-worker, ended up typing all 30 of them in an excel sheet, so we could analyse, sort and write notes next to them

posts.author: cherylbenke (Cheryl Benkert)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a great idea! I would totally use this for our company!

posts.author: Dly215 (Duong )
posts.cookedHtml: Great idea, especially since my organization relies on sometimes 30-40 automations (which i know is on the lower end even for some other orgs), and it’s such a nightmare trying to scroll through everything to find that one specific automation.

posts.author: JonnyS (Jonathan Stotlar)
posts.cookedHtml: I love this idea as well. I’m building multiple unique boards and being able to manage them on a higher level across projects would be great and save a ton of manual work.

posts.author: DandG (Graham Palmer)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree, it is a real pain having to manually enter them into a spreadsheet so that I can track them. I wonder how developers manage when they work on a legacy system. Any developers here?

posts.author: akm (angelica kemper)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad to see this suggestion was already published - I feel it would be hugely beneficial to have the ability to drag & drop board automations for sorting/organizing. I tend to label mine, create an index so I can reference what each custom automation recipe is but then end up getting out of order and forgetting about what I might be missing and/or have duplicates of. Especially as you get into 25+ automation recipes.

posts.author: aholsomback (Andrea)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely agree. At the very least it should default to auto sort by the Description. This way you can name it whatever you want to get the sort you’re looking for.

posts.author: pedro.diaz (Pedro Diaz Jr.)
posts.cookedHtml: Great Idea! I was trying to print my 50+ automations to manage/condense my list.
